title: Galianthe valerianoides
taxonomy: Galianthe valerianoides is classified within the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family consists of over 600 genera and 13,000 species, many of which are found in tropical and subtropical regions of the world. Galianthe valerianoides is a species of flowering plant that is native to South America, specifically in the Andes mountain range. It belongs to the genus Galianthe, which is a group of flowering plants that are native to the Americas and are often found in open, sunny areas. Within the genus Galianthe, Galianthe valerianoides is considered a distinct species due to its unique characteristics and morphology.
uses: Galianthe valerianoides is a plant species that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. However, there is limited information available on its traditional uses.
